intro.uz: Bu dars eʼtiqodlar haqida: AJ Hoge kuchsizlantiruvchi eʼtiqodlarni (limiting beliefs) kuch beruvchi eʼtiqodlarga (empowering beliefs) almashtirishni oʻrgatadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi ham shu gʻoyaga bogʻlangan: artikllar (a/an va the — yangi va tanish narsa) hamda fikr feʼllari va ‘that’ ergash gapi (I believe/think/know that…).
intro.en: This lesson is about beliefs: AJ Hoge teaches you to replace limiting beliefs with empowering ones. This week's two grammar topics fit that idea: articles (a/an vs the — first mention vs known) and mental-state verbs with that-clauses (I believe/think/know that…).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida biror narsani birinchi marta (a/an) va tanish holatda (the) toʻgʻri kirita olasiz, hamda oʻz eʼtiqodingizni “I believe that…”, “I think that…” qolipida toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can introduce something on first mention (a/an) and refer to it once known (the), and state your own beliefs with “I believe that…”, “I think that…” without pausing.

grammar.titleUz: Artikllar 1: a/an va the — yangi va tanish
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS — accurate article use is a visible marker of range and control
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can use a/an for first mention and the for known reference correctly
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida artikl umuman yoʻq — “eʼtiqod” soʻzi yolgʻiz turaveradi. Ingliz tili esa majburan koʻrsatadi: yangi/noaniq narsa → a/an (bir eʼtiqod), tanish/aniq narsa → the (oʻsha eʼtiqod). a yoki an tanlash harfga emas, TOVUSHga qarab: a university (yu-), an hour (h jim).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I have belief. She is best student. ✅ I have a belief. She is the best student. Sanaladigan birlik soʻz artiklsiz qololmaydi; birinchi marta → a/an, yagona yoki tanish → the. Umumiy koʻplik esa artiklsiz: “Beliefs are powerful.”

examples.en: George had a problem, and the problem was serious.
examples.uz: Jorjda bir muammo bor edi, va oʻsha muammo jiddiy edi.

examples.en: I have an empowering belief about English.
examples.uz: Menda ingliz tili haqida bir kuch beruvchi eʼtiqod bor.

examples.en: The belief that English is difficult is just an opinion.
examples.uz: “Ingliz tili qiyin” degan eʼtiqod — bu shunchaki bir fikr.

exercises.prompt: I have ___ empowering belief: English is easy.
exercises.hintUz: unli tovush (e-) oldidan → an; birinchi marta eslatilyapti

exercises.prompt: ___ belief that English is painful is only a memory.
It took ___ hour to change her mind.
exercises.hintUz: hour da h jim, unli tovush bilan boshlanadi → an

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda ovoz chiqarib ayting: avval a/an bilan bitta eʼtiqodni kiriting, keyin the bilan davom ettiring — “I have a belief. The belief is …”.
exercises.hintUz: birinchi marta → a/an; keyin oʻsha narsa → the

grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — give and support opinions (I believe that…, I think that…)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can express opinions and beliefs using I believe/think/know (that)…
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada fikr oldin, feʼl oxirida keladi: “Gapira olaman DEB ishonaman”. Ingliz tilida aksincha — avval feʼl, keyin fikr: “I believe THAT I can speak.” Oʻzbekcha “… deb” = inglizcha “that”, lekin u gap oxirida emas, oʻrtasida turadi (va nutqda koʻpincha tushib qoladi).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I think English isn’t difficult. ✅ I don’t think English is difficult. Ingliz tili inkorni asosiy feʼlga koʻchiradi (don’t think / don’t believe), ergash gap ijobiy qoladi.

examples.en: I believe that I can speak English fluently.
examples.uz: Ingliz tilida ravon gapira olishimga ishonaman.

examples.en: I think that language learning is natural.
I don’t think it is complicated.

exercises.prompt: I believe ___ my brain is a language machine.
exercises.hintUz: fikr feʼlidan keyin ergash gap → that (nutqda tushishi ham mumkin)
inkor asosiy feʼlga (don’t think); ergash gap ijobiy qoladi → is

exercises.prompt: Steve ___ that language learning is easy.

exercises.promptUz: Ikki kuch beruvchi eʼtiqodingizni ovoz chiqarib ayting: “I believe that … . I know that … .”

vocab.defEn: a deep emotional hurt from a painful experience
vocab.example: Many learners have emotional trauma about English.

vocab.defEn: a belief that stops you from improving
vocab.example: “I’m not good at English” is a limiting belief.

vocab.defEn: a belief that makes you feel powerful
vocab.example: “English can be fun” is an empowering belief.

vocab.defEn: to give power or confidence to someone
a phrase you repeat again and again, almost like magic
vocab.example: His incantation was “I’m handsome and I’m cool.”

vocab.defEn: a specific experience or example that supports a belief
vocab.example: You use references to prove a belief.

vocab.defEn: to ask about something to test if it is true
vocab.example: Question the references and the belief gets weaker.

vocab.defEn: to remove or throw away something you don’t want
vocab.example: You have to get rid of your limiting beliefs.

vocab.defEn: having many parts and difficult to understand
vocab.example: English feels complicated only if you study it that way.

vocab.defEn: enjoyable and requiring no hard struggle
vocab.example: English can be fun and effortless.

englishpod.title: The Office – Interview Skills 5: Discussing Reasons

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebekka ish suhbatida oʻz tajribasini tushuntiradi: avvaliga qiynalgan, ammo “slowly but surely” oʻzini isbotlagan. Bu — “Beliefs” mavzusiga mos: oʻziga boʻlgan ishonch odamni oldinga suradi. Sizningcha, Rebekka ishga qabul qilinadimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca explains her work history in a job interview: it was tough at first, but slowly but surely she proved herself. It fits the “Beliefs” theme — self-belief moves you forward. Do you think Rebecca gets the job?

keyVocab.defEn: causing feelings of discouragement or annoyance
happening gradually but inevitably, without fail
to show that you have the ability or character expected
a move to a better or more important position
covering a relatively long period of time
a demanding but stimulating test of your abilities

quiz.q: Which of these self-help books was published first?
quiz.qUz: Quyidagi oʻz-oʻziga yordam kitoblaridan qaysi biri birinchi nashr etilgan?
options: How to Win Friends and Influence People
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) How to Win Friends and Influence People — Deyl Karnegining bu kitobi 1936-yilda chiqqan. Think and Grow Rich esa 1937-yilda. Rob a) dedi va toʻgʻri topdi.

vocab.defEn: the activity of improving yourself, often through books and courses
something new that is popular for a period of time
making you feel more relaxed and less anxious
becoming smaller or weaker over time

vocab.en: to go from strength to strength
vocab.defEn: to stay strong or become even stronger
a person born between the mid-1980s and early 2000s

funEnglish.title: The Power of Belief: Mindset and Success

speakingPrompt.en: Talk about a belief you have about learning English. Was it a limiting belief or an empowering one? What do you believe now — and why?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: artikllar (a limiting belief, the belief that…) va fikr feʼllari + that (I believe that…, I think that…, I know that…).
